response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and testing craftsmanship, but the resume shows no experience in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU or accelerator architecture, or deep-learning workload optimization — the core requirements of this principal-level performance architect role. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and some embedded work, which does not substitute for silicon-level performance architecture expertise.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience, exceeding the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or performance-modeling experience
No AI/deep-learning training or inference optimization background
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator workload analysis exposure

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload background
Years of experience and systems performance fundamentals
